_description: Deterministic drug interaction rules for MedSafe Phase 2. Each rule is sourced from published clinical literature with verifiable citations.
_architecture_note: These rules are PARAMETERISED by the Phase 1 patient risk profile. The same rule may produce different verdicts for different patients based on their profile fields (eGFR, age, concurrent medications, conditions).

rules.description: NSAID contraindicated when eGFR < 60 mL/min/1.73m2
rules.reason_template: NSAID inappropriate in CKD stage {ckd_stage} (eGFR {egfr})
rules.citation: NICE NG203 §1.3.2 — Avoid nephrotoxic drugs in people with CKD; BNF: NSAIDs avoid in severe renal impairment

rules.description: NSAID + ACE-I/ARB + Diuretic = acute kidney injury risk (triple whammy)
rules.reason_template: NSAID + {ace_arb_name} + {diuretic_name} = triple-whammy AKI risk
rules.citation: Lapi et al., BMJ 2013;346:e8525 — Current use of triple therapy associated with 31% increased rate of AKI; Thomas MC, Nephrology 2000 — 'triple whammy' pattern

rules.description: Chronic NSAID use in patients aged 65 or older (Beers criteria)
rules.reason_template: Age {age} — Beers criteria: avoid chronic NSAIDs in patients ≥65 (increased GI bleeding, renal impairment, CV risk)
rules.citation: American Geriatrics Society 2023 Updated AGS Beers Criteria, JAGS 2023;71(7):2052-2077 — Table 2: NSAIDs

rules.description: NSAID + anticoagulant increases bleeding risk
rules.reason_template: NSAID + {anticoagulant_name} = significantly increased GI and other bleeding risk
rules.citation: BNF — NSAID interaction with warfarin: severity SEVERE; NICE CG141 — avoid concomitant use where possible

rules.description: NSAID reduces renal clearance of methotrexate, increasing toxicity risk
rules.reason_template: NSAID + methotrexate = reduced methotrexate clearance, increased toxicity risk (pancytopenia, hepatotoxicity)
rules.citation: BNF — NSAIDs reduce excretion of methotrexate: severity SEVERE; Stockley's Drug Interactions 12th ed — pp.795-797
rules.additional_context: Short-course NSAID with specialist supervision may be acceptable in rheumatology — this is a valid override scenario

rules.description: NSAIDs reduce renal clearance of lithium, risk of toxicity
rules.reason_template: NSAID + lithium = reduced lithium clearance, risk of toxicity (levels can rise 15-30%)
rules.citation: BNF — NSAIDs reduce excretion of lithium: severity SEVERE; Stockley's Drug Interactions 12th ed

rules.description: NSAID + SSRI increases GI bleeding risk
rules.reason_template: NSAID + SSRI = increased GI bleeding risk (both impair platelet function via different mechanisms)
rules.citation: de Abajo et al., BMJ 1999;319:1106 — combined use increases upper GI bleeding risk 15-fold vs neither drug

rules.description: ACE-I/ARB + potassium supplement = hyperkalaemia risk
rules.reason_template: Potassium supplement + {ace_arb_name} = hyperkalaemia risk (ACE-I/ARBs reduce potassium excretion)
rules.citation: BNF — ACE inhibitors increase risk of hyperkalaemia with potassium salts: severity SEVERE

rules.description: ACE-I/ARB + spironolactone = significant hyperkalaemia risk
rules.reason_template: Spironolactone + {ace_arb_name} = significant hyperkalaemia risk — requires close monitoring of potassium
rules.citation: NICE CG187 — monitor potassium within 1 week of starting combination; RALES trial safety data
rules.additional_context: Combination is used therapeutically in heart failure but requires monitoring. Not an absolute contraindication.

rules.description: Simvastatin/atorvastatin + macrolide antibiotic = increased myopathy/rhabdomyolysis risk
rules.reason_template: {macrolide_name} + {statin_name} = increased statin levels via CYP3A4 inhibition, rhabdomyolysis risk
rules.citation: BNF — Clarithromycin increases concentration of simvastatin: severity SEVERE; MHRA Drug Safety Update 2012
rules.additional_context: Consider temporarily withholding statin during short antibiotic course, or use azithromycin (weaker CYP3A4 inhibitor)

rules.description: Warfarin + macrolide = increased INR and bleeding risk
rules.reason_template: {macrolide_name} + warfarin = increased INR via CYP inhibition and altered gut flora — monitor INR closely
rules.citation: BNF — Macrolides increase anticoagulant effect of coumarins: severity SEVERE

rules.description: Warfarin + trimethoprim = significantly increased INR
rules.reason_template: Trimethoprim + warfarin = significant INR increase (mean rise 40-65%) — high bleeding risk
rules.citation: Glasheen et al., J Gen Intern Med 2005;20:653-656; BNF — Trimethoprim enhances anticoagulant effect of coumarins: severity SEVERE

rules.description: Metformin contraindicated when eGFR < 30
rules.reason_template: Metformin contraindicated at eGFR {egfr} (<30) — lactic acidosis risk
rules.citation: NICE NG28 §1.6.1 — Stop metformin if eGFR <30; BNF — manufacturer advises avoid

rules.description: Metformin requires dose reduction when eGFR 30-45
rules.reason_template: Metformin: review dose at eGFR {egfr} (<45) — reduce to max 1g/day; stop if <30
rules.citation: NICE NG28 §1.6.1 — Review metformin dose if eGFR <45

rules.description: Gabapentin/pregabalin requires dose reduction in renal impairment
rules.reason_template: Gabapentinoid dose adjustment required at eGFR {egfr} (<50) — renally cleared, accumulation risk
rules.citation: BNF — reduce dose in renal impairment; manufacturer SPCs for gabapentin and pregabalin

rules.description: ACE-I + ARB dual blockade — increased renal and hyperkalaemia risk without proven benefit
rules.reason_template: Dual RAAS blockade (ACE-I + ARB) — no additional benefit, increased AKI and hyperkalaemia risk
rules.citation: ONTARGET trial, NEJM 2008;358:1547; VA NEPHRON-D trial — stopped early for harm; NICE NG203 advises against

rules.description: NSAID caution when eGFR 45-60 (CKD stage 3a)
rules.reason_template: NSAID: caution at eGFR {egfr} — use lowest effective dose for shortest duration; monitor renal function
rules.citation: NICE NG203 §1.3.2; BNF — use with caution in mild-moderate renal impairment

rules.description: NSAID in heart failure — fluid retention and worsening
rules.reason_template: NSAID in heart failure — causes fluid retention, can precipitate acute decompensation
rules.citation: NICE NG106 §1.5 — Avoid NSAIDs in heart failure; ESC 2021 HF guidelines

rules.description: NSAID in patient with GI bleed/ulcer history
rules.reason_template: NSAID in patient with GI bleed/ulcer history — high risk of recurrence; if essential, co-prescribe PPI
rules.citation: NICE CG141 — assess and manage GI risk; co-prescribe PPI if NSAID required in high-risk patients

rules.description: Penicillin allergy — consider cephalosporin cross-reactivity
rules.reason_template: Patient has penicillin allergy — cephalosporin cross-reactivity risk ~1-2% (higher with 1st-gen cephalosporins)
rules.citation: Campagna et al., J Emerg Med 2012;42:612-620 — cross-reactivity approximately 1-2%; Pichichero, NEJM 2006

rules.description: NSAID allergy cross-reactivity between different NSAIDs
rules.reason_template: Patient has documented NSAID allergy/intolerance — cross-reactivity between NSAIDs is common (COX-1 mediated)
rules.citation: BNF — patients with NSAID hypersensitivity: avoid all NSAIDs; NICE CG177

rules.description: ACE-I in context of rapid renal decline — may need review
rules.reason_template: ACE-I with rapid eGFR decline ({rate}/month) — consider whether ACE-I is contributing to haemodynamic decline; nephrology review may be indicated
rules.citation: NICE NG203 §1.4 — Refer if eGFR decline >5 mL/min/year or >25% in 12 months; Kidney Disease: Improving Global Outcomes (KDIGO) 2024
